response:
We found them by pulling the raw Service Bus operational logs from the storage account they were being forwarded to and matching the tracking id to the operational logs.

Steps taken:
- Noted repeated Service Bus 40100 (Unauthorized) GetSubscription errors in the prod-azure-v3 namespace.
- Discovered Service Bus operational logs were forwarded to Datadog via a storage account (not into Log Analytics).
- Opened the raw blob logs in storage account ddlogstorage3a0c161f2e70 and searched for the offending request payload.
- Located the exact payload (TrackingId 7b845798-c613-49fa-9cc3-c49ca97cce31_G17) and then matched it to the insights-logs-operationallogs record for 2026-04-15 hour 16.
- From that payload / log record we identified the client pattern and therefore the likely source application causing the 40100 errors.

→ x-auth-ddi"], "recent": ["2026-04-15: On 2026-04-15, traced Service Bus 40100 unauthorized GetSubscription errors in namespace prod-azure-v3 to a specific client pattern and likely source app. Service Bus operational logs were only configured to Datadog forwarding storage, not Log Analytics, so the raw blob logs in storage account ddlogstorage3a0c161f2e70 had to be searched. The exact payload with TrackingId 7b845798-c613-49fa-9cc3-c49ca97cce31_G17 was found in insights-logs-operationallogs for 2026-04-15 hour 16. Matching record: E", "2026-04-15: On 2026-04-15, investigation of fazure-mcp 500/502/403 traffic found that the current issue was not WAF regression and not backend 500s. In AGWAccessLogs for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com over the prior 24h, there were 0 HTTP 500 responses, multiple 502s concentrated around 13:46-13:50 UTC and 14:14-14:15 UTC and especially 14:38-14:51 UTC, and a small number of 403s. AGWFirewallLogs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and current 403s were mainly expected", "2026-04-03: On 2026-04-03 in fleet-platform-app prod telemetry, investigated two incidents during a 60-minute deployment watch. Main app incident: between 17:33:55Z and 17:34:20Z, send_bulk_operation_callback failed with RetryableHTTPError HTTP 500 after 4 attempts from BULK_AUTOMATION_CALLBACK_URL=https://governance.mscloud.rackspace.com/policy-engine/v2/bulk-operations/callback. Multiple distinct returned correlationIds were observed (including 9e3e8b0f-43ce-4c05-8885-1c8b48dc88be and 7c189fbd-4a41-45b1-a", "2026-04-01: April 1, 2026 prod App Insights triage for fleet platform: main app azr-fleet-platform-prod showed no active exceptions or failed requests in the queried 24h window for resource_change_ingest_sb_trigger after prior fixes. Remaining active failures were in azr-fleet-webhook-prod: 4 recent WebhookEnqueueFailure 503s caused by transient Microsoft.ServiceBus send timeouts during sender.send_messages. Patched functions-webhook/function_app.py to retry once on transient send timeout using a fresh reti", "2026-03-20: On 2026-03-20, live Azure investigation of Log Analytics workspace DefaultWorkspace-5ad08c08-96bb-45c0-a567-8bcc86020661-SCUS in subscription 5ad08c08-96bb-45c0-a567-8bcc86020661 (Azure Engineering - Production Subscription) found AppExceptions billed usage of 1552.998 GB over 90 days. 95.5% of this was from App Insights component prod-azure-audit-engine / Function App prod-azure-audit-engine in resource group ussc-prod-audit-engine-rg001. Dominant signature: Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.ServiceBu", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19, read-only inspection of the prod Service Bus topic subscription azr-fleet-platform-events/automation/catchall correlated visible topic messages to governance callback 404s in azr-compliance-v3. Catchall active backlog had 3697 messages and 1223 dead-letter messages; dead-letter items were only TTLExpiredException from 2026-03-01 to 2026-03-05, unrelated to the incident. Incident-hour correlation: 2026-03-17T02:00Z had 4 catchall messages and 4 callback 404s; all 4 were policy-labe", "2026-03-20: Webhook incident on March 20, 2026: prod webhook ingress was healthy and Service Bus auth worked, but main function apps in dev/prod were missing the new resource_change_ingest_sb_trigger live even though code/app settings had been updated. Root cause was trigger metadata not synced for the main app after adding a new Service Bus trigger; manual POST to Microsoft.Web/sites/<app>/syncfunctiontriggers immediately surfaced resource_change_ingest_sb_trigger in both dev and prod. After sync, resource", "2026-03-20: On 2026-03-20 during prod health validation of fleet-platform-app after PR #59 deployment, azr-fleet-platform-prod main app was healthy but azr-fleet-webhook-prod was in an active outage. Public and direct webhook health endpoints returned 500, while direct main /api/health returned 200. App Insights over the prior 30 minutes showed about 71,770 webhook requests with about 70,524 failures. Dominant exceptions were System.InvalidOperationException 'Did not find any initialized language workers' a", "2026-03-19: For the 2026-03-19 azr-compliance-v3 prod callback 404 investigation, the python-httpx client was traced to the external Fleet Platform prod function app, not the compliance repo backend. Evidence: the shared ASE outbound IP 172.206.172.169 is used by multiple apps, but azr-fleet-platform-prod and azr-fleet-webhook-prod are the only prod Fleet Python function apps. The automation Service Bus topic in namespace azr-fleet-platform-events has semantic subscriptions policy/alerts/ama/tags; the polic", "2026-03-19: In azr-compliance-v3 prod callback 404 investigation on 2026-03-19, APIM and App Insights confirmed the 451 bulk callback 404s were not gateway misses or WAF blocks. AGWAccessLogs showed one client IP 172.206.172.169 with user agent python-httpx/0.28.1 hitting governance.mscloud.rackspace.com/policy-engine/v2/bulk-operations/callback and receiving 404.
